sections.title: Built for founders building inside systems not built for them.
body: Cupcakes + Broccoli exists for founders who need steady thinking, structural awareness, and support grounded in real experience inside complex institutions and real businesses.

image.alt: Amanda Johnson in a studio portrait.

sections.title: Amanda Johnson
body: Amanda Johnson began her career at NBC News, working inside the network newsroom at 30 Rockefeller Plaza in New York.

After earning a degree in Industrial Engineering from Cornell University and a Master's in Broadcast Journalism from Syracuse University, she joined NBC, producing live national segments, covering presidential campaigns, and operating inside one of the most demanding environments in network news.
That newsroom foundation shaped how she thinks: quickly, structurally, and with clarity under pressure.
She later earned her MBA in Marketing from NYU Stern.

From there, her work expanded into executive marketing and brand leadership across media, beauty, and consumer brands. She has led large cross-channel campaigns, managed significant operating budgets, relaunched global brand platforms, built revenue-driving strategies for established companies and emerging ventures, raised multimillion-dollar sponsorships, helped scale startups, and advised teams navigating high-stakes growth and change.
She understands how large institutions think because she has operated inside them.

image.alt: Amanda Johnson smiling in a studio portrait.

sections.title: Structure, support, and strategic clarity for founders.
body: Cupcakes + Broccoli is for founders building inside systems that were not built for them: corporate systems, capital systems, and media systems.
Inside institutions, strategy is reviewed, risks are tested, and decisions are rarely made alone. Outside them, founders are expected to move fast and absorb the consequences.
This work gives founders access to that same level of structure and strategic support.
It blends creativity with discipline, vision with structure, and ambition with operational clarity.

image.alt: Guests talking across a shared table.

sections.title: Ownership builds leverage, options, and long-term stability.
body: Building something that lasts requires steady thinking, structural awareness, and the confidence to move through systems that were not designed for you.
